title: What is the Temporal Dead Zone?
options: The period between entering scope and variable declaration where let/const variables cannot be accessed
correct_answer: The period between entering scope and variable declaration where let/const variables cannot be accessed

title: What is Symbol in JavaScript?
description: What is the Symbol primitive type?
options: A unique, immutable primitive used as object property keys to avoid name collisions
correct_answer: A unique, immutable primitive used as object property keys to avoid name collisions

title: What is the Symbol.iterator?
options: Defines the default iterator for an object, enabling it to be used in for...of loops
correct_answer: Defines the default iterator for an object, enabling it to be used in for...of loops

title: What is a generator function?
options: Functions declared with function* that can pause and resume using yield, returning an iterator
correct_answer: Functions declared with function* that can pause and resume using yield, returning an iterator

title: What is the yield keyword?
description: What does yield do in a generator?
options: Pauses the generator and returns a value; execution resumes on next() call
correct_answer: Pauses the generator and returns a value; execution resumes on next() call

title: What is the Proxy object?
options: Creates a proxy that intercepts and customizes fundamental operations on objects (get, set, delete, etc.)
correct_answer: Creates a proxy that intercepts and customizes fundamental operations on objects (get, set, delete, etc.)

title: What is the Reflect API?
options: Methods for interceptable JavaScript operations, often used with Proxy for default behavior
correct_answer: Methods for interceptable JavaScript operations, often used with Proxy for default behavior

title: What is WeakRef?
options: Holds a weak reference to an object, allowing it to be garbage collected if no other references exist
correct_answer: Holds a weak reference to an object, allowing it to be garbage collected if no other references exist

title: What is FinalizationRegistry?
options: Allows registration of a callback when a registered object is garbage collected
correct_answer: Allows registration of a callback when a registered object is garbage collected

title: What is structuredClone?
options: Creates a deep clone of a value using the structured clone algorithm, handling circular references
correct_answer: Creates a deep clone of a value using the structured clone algorithm, handling circular references

title: What is optional chaining?
options: Safely accesses nested properties, returning undefined instead of throwing if a reference is nullish
correct_answer: Safely accesses nested properties, returning undefined instead of throwing if a reference is nullish

title: What is nullish coalescing?
options: Returns the right operand when the left is null or undefined (not for other falsy values like 0 or '')
correct_answer: Returns the right operand when the left is null or undefined (not for other falsy values like 0 or '')

title: What is the logical assignment operator?
options: Assigns the right value only if the left is null or undefined: x ??= y
correct_answer: Assigns the right value only if the left is null or undefined: x ??= y

title: What is Array.at()?
description: What does the at() method do?
options: Returns the element at a given index, supporting negative indices to count from the end
correct_answer: Returns the element at a given index, supporting negative indices to count from the end

title: What is Object.hasOwn()?
options: Returns true if the object has the specified property as its own (not inherited), replacing hasOwnProperty
correct_answer: Returns true if the object has the specified property as its own (not inherited), replacing hasOwnProperty

title: What are private class fields?
description: How do you declare private fields in JS classes?
options: Using the # prefix: #privateField; they are not accessible outside the class
correct_answer: Using the # prefix: #privateField; they are not accessible outside the class

title: What is the static keyword in classes?
description: What does static do in a class?
options: Defines a method or property on the class itself, not on instances
correct_answer: Defines a method or property on the class itself, not on instances

title: What is the super keyword?
description: What does super do in a class?
options: Calls the parent class constructor or methods
correct_answer: Calls the parent class constructor or methods

title: What is Object.freeze?
options: Makes an object immutable: no adding, removing, or modifying properties (shallow)
correct_answer: Makes an object immutable: no adding, removing, or modifying properties (shallow)

title: What is Object.seal?
description: How does Object.seal differ from Object.freeze?
options: Seal prevents adding/removing properties but allows modifying existing ones; freeze prevents all changes
correct_answer: Seal prevents adding/removing properties but allows modifying existing ones; freeze prevents all changes

title: What is the event loop?
description: How does the JavaScript event loop work?
options: Continuously checks if the call stack is empty, then processes tasks from the microtask queue first, then the macrotask queue
correct_answer: Continuously checks if the call stack is empty, then processes tasks from the microtask queue first, then the macrotask queue

title: What is the difference between microtasks and macrotasks?
options: Microtasks (Promise callbacks, queueMicrotask) execute before macrotasks (setTimeout, setInterval) after each task
correct_answer: Microtasks (Promise callbacks, queueMicrotask) execute before macrotasks (setTimeout, setInterval) after each task

title: What is queueMicrotask?
options: Queues a function to run as a microtask, after the current task but before the next macrotask
correct_answer: Queues a function to run as a microtask, after the current task but before the next macrotask

title: What is requestAnimationFrame?
options: Schedules a callback before the next browser repaint (~60fps), ideal for smooth animations
correct_answer: Schedules a callback before the next browser repaint (~60fps), ideal for smooth animations

title: What is the AbortController?
options: Allows aborting one or more web requests (fetch, event listeners) via an AbortSignal
correct_answer: Allows aborting one or more web requests (fetch, event listeners) via an AbortSignal

title: What is the Intl API?
description: What does the Intl object provide?
options: Internationalization API for language-sensitive string comparison, number formatting, date formatting
correct_answer: Internationalization API for language-sensitive string comparison, number formatting, date formatting

title: What is a SharedArrayBuffer?
options: A fixed-length binary buffer that can be shared between the main thread and Web Workers
correct_answer: A fixed-length binary buffer that can be shared between the main thread and Web Workers

title: What is Atomics in JavaScript?
description: What does the Atomics object provide?
options: Static methods for atomic operations on SharedArrayBuffer, ensuring thread-safe access
correct_answer: Static methods for atomic operations on SharedArrayBuffer, ensuring thread-safe access

title: What is the difference between var, let, and const?
description: How do var, let, and const differ in scoping?
options: var is function-scoped and hoisted; let and const are block-scoped with TDZ; const cannot be reassigned
correct_answer: var is function-scoped and hoisted; let and const are block-scoped with TDZ; const cannot be reassigned

title: What is hoisting?
description: How does hoisting work for var and functions?
options: Variable and function declarations are moved to the top of their scope during compilation; var is initialized to undefined, functions are fully hoisted
Nothing is hoisted in strict mode
correct_answer: Variable and function declarations are moved to the top of their scope during compilation; var is initialized to undefined, functions are fully hoisted

title: What is the typeof operator?
options: A string indicating the type of the operand (e.g., 'string', 'number', 'object', 'undefined')
correct_answer: A string indicating the type of the operand (e.g., 'string', 'number', 'object', 'undefined')

title: Why is typeof null === 'object'?
options: It's a historical bug from the first JavaScript implementation that checked type tags; null's tag was 0 (same as objects)
correct_answer: It's a historical bug from the first JavaScript implementation that checked type tags; null's tag was 0 (same as objects)

title: What is the difference between null and undefined?
description: How do null and undefined differ?
options: undefined means a variable has been declared but not assigned; null is an intentional assignment of 'no value'
correct_answer: undefined means a variable has been declared but not assigned; null is an intentional assignment of 'no value'

title: What is destructuring assignment?
options: Unpacks values from arrays or properties from objects into distinct variables
correct_answer: Unpacks values from arrays or properties from objects into distinct variables

title: What is the spread operator?
options: Expands an iterable into individual elements (for arrays, objects, function args)
correct_answer: Expands an iterable into individual elements (for arrays, objects, function args)

title: What is the rest parameter?
description: What does ...rest do in function parameters?
options: Collects all remaining arguments into an array
correct_answer: Collects all remaining arguments into an array

title: What is template literal?
description: What are template literals used for?
options: String literals using backticks that allow embedded expressions (${expr}) and multi-line strings
correct_answer: String literals using backticks that allow embedded expressions (${expr}) and multi-line strings

title: What is tagged template literal?
options: A function called with a template literal, receiving the string parts and expression values separately
correct_answer: A function called with a template literal, receiving the string parts and expression values separately

title: What is the for...of loop?
options: Values of iterable objects (arrays, strings, Maps, Sets, generators)
correct_answer: Values of iterable objects (arrays, strings, Maps, Sets, generators)

title: What is the for...in loop?
options: Enumerable string properties of an object (including inherited ones)
correct_answer: Enumerable string properties of an object (including inherited ones)

title: Why avoid for...in on arrays?
description: What problems can for...in cause with arrays?
options: It iterates over all enumerable properties including inherited ones, indices are strings, and order isn't guaranteed
correct_answer: It iterates over all enumerable properties including inherited ones, indices are strings, and order isn't guaranteed

title: What is the Map object?
description: How does Map differ from a plain object?
options: Map allows any type as keys, maintains insertion order, has a size property, and is iterable
Map is the same as Object
correct_answer: Map allows any type as keys, maintains insertion order, has a size property, and is iterable

title: What is the Set object?
options: A collection of unique values of any type, with O(1) has/add/delete
correct_answer: A collection of unique values of any type, with O(1) has/add/delete

title: What is WeakMap?
description: How does WeakMap differ from Map?
options: WeakMap keys must be objects, keys are held weakly (garbage collectible), and it's not iterable
correct_answer: WeakMap keys must be objects, keys are held weakly (garbage collectible), and it's not iterable

title: What is WeakSet?
description: How does WeakSet differ from Set?
options: WeakSet only stores objects, holds them weakly (garbage collectible), and is not iterable
correct_answer: WeakSet only stores objects, holds them weakly (garbage collectible), and is not iterable

title: What is Promise.allSettled?
description: How does Promise.allSettled differ from Promise.all?
options: allSettled waits for all promises to settle (resolve or reject) and returns all results; all short-circuits on first rejection
correct_answer: allSettled waits for all promises to settle (resolve or reject) and returns all results; all short-circuits on first rejection

title: What is Promise.any?
options: Returns the first fulfilled promise; throws AggregateError if all reject
correct_answer: Returns the first fulfilled promise; throws AggregateError if all reject

title: What is Promise.race?
options: Returns the first promise to settle (either fulfilled or rejected)
correct_answer: Returns the first promise to settle (either fulfilled or rejected)

title: What is async iteration?
options: Iterates over async iterables, awaiting each value in the iteration
correct_answer: Iterates over async iterables, awaiting each value in the iteration

title: What is the globalThis object?
options: A standard way to access the global object across environments (window, self, global, or globalThis)
correct_answer: A standard way to access the global object across environments (window, self, global, or globalThis)

title: What is the import() expression?
options: Dynamically imports a module at runtime, returning a Promise, enabling code splitting
correct_answer: Dynamically imports a module at runtime, returning a Promise, enabling code splitting

title: What is the import.meta object?
options: Context-specific metadata about the current module, such as import.meta.url
correct_answer: Context-specific metadata about the current module, such as import.meta.url

title: What is top-level await?
options: Using await at the top level of ES modules without wrapping in an async function
Awaiting in global scope of scripts
correct_answer: Using await at the top level of ES modules without wrapping in an async function

title: What is the RegExp named capture group?
description: How do named capture groups work?
options: (?<name>pattern) creates a named group accessible via match.groups.name
correct_answer: (?<name>pattern) creates a named group accessible via match.groups.name

title: What is the flat() method?
options: Creates a new array with all sub-array elements concatenated recursively up to a specified depth
correct_answer: Creates a new array with all sub-array elements concatenated recursively up to a specified depth

title: What is the flatMap() method?
options: Maps each element using a mapping function, then flattens the result by one level
correct_answer: Maps each element using a mapping function, then flattens the result by one level

title: What is Object.fromEntries?
options: Transforms a list of key-value pairs into an object (inverse of Object.entries)
correct_answer: Transforms a list of key-value pairs into an object (inverse of Object.entries)

title: What is the label statement?
description: What do labels do in JavaScript?
options: Provide a reference identifier for break/continue statements to control nested loops
correct_answer: Provide a reference identifier for break/continue statements to control nested loops

title: What is Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor?
options: Returns the property descriptor (value, writable, enumerable, configurable) for an own property
correct_answer: Returns the property descriptor (value, writable, enumerable, configurable) for an own property

title: What is tail call optimization?
options: An optimization where a function call in tail position reuses the current stack frame, preventing stack overflow (only in strict mode, Safari)
correct_answer: An optimization where a function call in tail position reuses the current stack frame, preventing stack overflow (only in strict mode, Safari)

title: Implement a deep equality check.
description: Write a function that deeply compares two values for equality, handling objects, arrays, and primitives.

title: Implement Array.prototype.reduce.
Implement Promise.all.

description: Write a function that retries an async operation up to n times with a delay between attempts.

title: Implement a simple pub/sub system.
description: Create a publish-subscribe event system with on, off, and emit methods.

title: Implement Function.prototype.bind.
Implement a simple Promise.
description: Create a basic Promise implementation with then and catch.

title: Implement a LazyIterator.
description: Create a lazy iterator class that supports map, filter, and take operations on arrays without creating intermediate arrays.

title: What is the difference between == and ===?
options: == performs type coercion before comparison; === checks both type and value without coercion
correct_answer: == performs type coercion before comparison; === checks both type and value without coercion

title: What is the 'this' keyword?
options: 'this' refers to the execution context: in a method it's the object, in a function it depends on how it's called, arrow functions inherit from parent scope
correct_answer: 'this' refers to the execution context: in a method it's the object, in a function it depends on how it's called, arrow functions inherit from parent scope

title: What is an arrow function?
description: How do arrow functions differ from regular functions?
options: No own 'this', no arguments object, cannot be used as constructors, and have concise syntax
correct_answer: No own 'this', no arguments object, cannot be used as constructors, and have concise syntax

title: What is a closure?
description: What is a closure in JavaScript?
options: A function that has access to variables from its outer (enclosing) scope even after the outer function has returned
correct_answer: A function that has access to variables from its outer (enclosing) scope even after the outer function has returned

title: What is an IIFE?
description: What is an Immediately Invoked Function Expression?
options: A function that is defined and executed immediately: (function() { })()
correct_answer: A function that is defined and executed immediately: (function() { })()

title: What is the prototype chain?
options: Objects have an internal [[Prototype]] link; property lookups traverse this chain until the property is found or null is reached
correct_answer: Objects have an internal [[Prototype]] link; property lookups traverse this chain until the property is found or null is reached

title: What is Object.create?
options: Creates a new object with the specified prototype object and optional property descriptors
correct_answer: Creates a new object with the specified prototype object and optional property descriptors

title: What is the arguments object?
description: What is the arguments object in functions?
options: An array-like object containing all arguments passed to a non-arrow function
correct_answer: An array-like object containing all arguments passed to a non-arrow function

title: What is strict mode?
options: Stricter parsing and error handling: prevents silent errors, disallows undeclared variables, etc.
correct_answer: Stricter parsing and error handling: prevents silent errors, disallows undeclared variables, etc.

title: What is the module pattern?
description: How does the module pattern work?
options: Uses closures via IIFE to create private scope, returning only a public API object
correct_answer: Uses closures via IIFE to create private scope, returning only a public API object

title: What is Event.preventDefault()?
options: Prevents the browser's default action for an event (e.g., form submission, link navigation)
correct_answer: Prevents the browser's default action for an event (e.g., form submission, link navigation)

title: What is Event.stopPropagation()?
options: Stops the event from bubbling up or capturing down the DOM tree
correct_answer: Stops the event from bubbling up or capturing down the DOM tree

title: What is event delegation?
options: Attaching a single event listener to a parent element to handle events from its children via bubbling
correct_answer: Attaching a single event listener to a parent element to handle events from its children via bubbling

title: What is the MutationObserver?
options: Watches for changes in the DOM tree (attributes, child list, subtree) and fires callbacks
correct_answer: Watches for changes in the DOM tree (attributes, child list, subtree) and fires callbacks

title: What is the IntersectionObserver?
options: Observes when an element enters or exits the viewport or a parent element, useful for lazy loading
Observes element intersections for collision detection
correct_answer: Observes when an element enters or exits the viewport or a parent element, useful for lazy loading

title: What is the ResizeObserver?
options: Reports changes to the dimensions of an element's content or border box
correct_answer: Reports changes to the dimensions of an element's content or border box

title: What is the Broadcast Channel API?
options: Enables communication between different tabs, windows, or iframes of the same origin
correct_answer: Enables communication between different tabs, windows, or iframes of the same origin

title: Implement Array.prototype.flat.
description: Write a polyfill for Array.prototype.flat with configurable depth.

title: Implement a compose function.
description: Write a function that composes multiple functions from right to left.
